stimulus.content: The New England colonies had long, cold winters and rocky soil. Farming was hard, so many colonists turned to fishing, shipbuilding, and trade to earn a living. Towns grew around harbors along the Atlantic coast.

stem: Why did many New England colonists turn to fishing and trade?

choices.text: They wanted to explore the open ocean
The rocky soil made farming difficult
The king ordered them to build ships
They had no rivers for fresh water

answer_explanation: The passage states that long winters and rocky soil made farming hard, so colonists turned to fishing, shipbuilding, and trade. The other choices are not supported by the passage.
